title: impatiens parviflora var. brachycentra
intro: Impatiens parviflora var. brachycentra, also known as the Balsam Impatiens, is a flowering plant that belongs to the balsam family and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Balsam Impatiens include reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and soothing respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
